summary: Completed comprehensive testing of FairFare Decision Engine. All features working correctly: 150-mile route validation, qualitative indicators (price_level, surge_likelihood), coordinate validation, geocoding from autocomplete, and Decision Engine UI.
passed_tests: Backend: API root endpoint returns 200 OK
Backend: Valid short route (Times Square to Central Park ~2 miles) accepted
Backend: Valid medium route (NYC to Philadelphia ~95 miles) accepted
Backend: Routes > 150 miles rejected with 400 error and clear message
Backend: Very long routes (NYC to LA) rejected
Backend: Missing pickup coordinates returns 400 error
Backend: Missing destination coordinates returns 400 error
Backend: Response includes price_level (Cheap/Moderate/Busy)
Backend: Response includes surge_likelihood (Low/Moderate/High)
Backend: Response includes decision_hint with contextual advice
Backend: Response includes availability (Good/Limited/Busy)
Backend: Response does NOT contain numeric price fields
Backend: Response includes deep links for Uber and Lyft
Backend: Deep links contain pickup and destination coordinates
Backend: Response includes distance_miles and duration_minutes
Backend: Response includes route_status
Backend: Response includes coordinate info
Frontend: Initial button shows 'Enter pickup location' when empty
Frontend: Button disabled when coordinates missing
Frontend: Button shows 'Select pickup from suggestions' when typed without selecting
Frontend: Autocomplete suggestions appear for pickup
Frontend: Autocomplete suggestions appear for destination
Frontend: Selecting from suggestions sets coordinates correctly
Frontend: Button enabled after selecting both locations from suggestions
Frontend: Compare Rides navigates to results view
Frontend: Decision hint displayed in results
Frontend: FairFare Pick card displayed
Frontend: Uber and Lyft cards displayed
Frontend: Price level indicators (Cheap/Moderate/Busy) with color coding
Frontend: Surge likelihood indicators (Low/Moderate/High) with color coding
Frontend: 'Live price shown in app' message displayed
Frontend: Continue buttons for Uber and Lyft present
Frontend: Refresh button present
Frontend: Back button present
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Decision Engine features verified working. Backend uses time-based heuristics for demand/surge indicators (MOCKED - no real Uber/Lyft API calls). Frontend properly validates coordinates before enabling Compare button. Geocoding uses OpenStreetMap Nominatim API.
mocked_api_note: Backend is entirely MOCKED - no real Uber/Lyft API calls. Decision engine uses time-based heuristics for demand/surge indicators.
